what_it_does: Defines the local CSS for the 'platform' UI kit — a full application-shell style sheet (sidebar + main grid, topbar, buttons, search, media tiles, forms, checkboxes, dropzone, a calendar widget, and a floating chats pill), layered on top of the shared colors_and_type.css token base.
observations: Header comment states it is 'local styles on top of colors_and_type.css'
Two-column app shell via CSS grid (280px sidebar + 1fr main), html/body/#app set to full height with overflow:hidden
Heavy use of design tokens via var(): --font-body/display/mono, --fg-*, --bg-*, --accent-gold*/bronze, --border-*, --r-* radii, --shadow-*, --ease-out
Extensive 'cv-' class namespace (cv-shell, cv-sidebar, cv-nav-item, cv-btn, cv-search, cv-tile, cv-panel, cv-cal-*, cv-chats, etc.)
Dashed-border motif used repeatedly for slots/placeholders (cv-logo-slot, cv-slot, cv-nav-item.active, cv-btn--dashed)
Calendar block (cv-cal-*) includes color-coded chips and task names by category

Several hardcoded hex colors break the token pattern: #F04325 (badge red, twice), #DCD5BC (dropzone hover), #FAF1B6 (add button), and the entire cal-task/cal-chip color palette (#5A8A4A green, #7E5AB8 purple, #C24A3C red, #4A78B8 blue, #E5A547 orange, #E5C547 yellow, #A89678 gray) plus chip backgrounds
Notification badges (cv-bell-badge, cv-chats .count) use fixed pill shapes (border-radius:999px)
standouts: The dashed-border placeholder aesthetic (logo slot, generic slots, active nav, dashed buttons) is a distinct visual signature
A self-contained calendar widget with a category color system lives inside this kit CSS
Gold accent is the dominant brand color, threaded everywhere via --accent-gold variants
conventions: Follows the 'reference the token, never the raw value' convention for most layout/brand colors
BREAKS that convention with ~15 hardcoded hex literals, especially the calendar category palette and alert reds — these are off-token literals the design-lint would flag
concerns: Hardcoded hex colors (#F04325 alert red repeated, #DCD5BC, #FAF1B6, and the full calendar category palette) are off-token and would drift from the central token source
Calendar category colors are defined inline per-class rather than as named tokens/roles, so a palette change needs editing this file in many places
notes: The platform kit's full app-shell stylesheet — mostly tokenised but carrying a cluster of hardcoded calendar/alert hex colors that sit outside the token system.
questions: Are the calendar category colors (green/purple/red/blue/orange) and the #F04325 alert red intended to become tokens, or are they accepted exceptions?
Does colors_and_type.css define all the var() tokens this file consumes, or are some undefined?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header says 'local styles on top of colors_and_type.css' and the file is exactly that: kit-local component styles referencing the shared token base.
intention_signals: Aims to give the 'platform' UI kit a complete, tokenised look for a full app shell so screens (Calendar, Gallery) can be composed from cv- classes without restating layout or brand values.
novelty: The dashed-border placeholder motif used as an intentional, recurring design language element, and an embedded calendar widget with a per-category color taxonomy.
contribution: Contributes a complete platform app-shell stylesheet (sidebar/main grid, button modifiers, search, media tiles, forms, dropzone, calendar widget, chats pill) and a dashed-placeholder visual signature, mostly bound to shared tokens.
summary_for_embedding: Platform UI kit CSS defining a tokenised app shell — sidebar, topbar, buttons, search, media tiles, forms, a calendar widget, and a chats pill — using cv- classes on top of colors_and_type.css.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the platform kit a complete, single-sourced visual style for a full application shell that screens compose from.
